channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: ISI Appoints Doug Caccese as CEO to Lead Next Phase of Growth
item.description: I’m confident he will be a tremendous addition to ISI and guide the company through its next phase of growth.” “ISI has built a reputation for being a true partner to its customers, and that reputation is what drew me here,” said Doug Caccese, Chief Executive Officer of ISI. For customers, it remains business as usual: they will continue working with the same trusted ISI team, while benefiting from additional executive experience to support the company’s next phase of growth and innovation. Doug Caccese has been appointed Chief Executive Officer of ISI.

item.title: NY AG Suing 3M, Other Chemical Companies Over PFAS Pollution
item.description: I look forward to ensuring the companies responsible for PFAS pollution are held accountable.” James alleges that the companies violated New York laws by producing, marketing and selling products containing chemicals they knew to be harmful to New Yorkers’ health and the environment for decades. New York Attorney General Letitia James on Thursday sued some of the nation’s largest chemical and agricultural companies — 3M Company, EIDP Inc., The Chemours Company Inc., Corteva Inc., and DuPont De Nemours Inc. — for contributing to decades of toxic polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) pollution in the state through their use in consumer products.

item.title: Soft P/C Market May Be Shallower Than Previous Cycles: Swiss Re
item.description: But Swiss Re said the current underwriting cycle may prove shallower than past soft markets as rising repair costs and growing catastrophe exposures limit pricing corrections. Swiss Re said the current cycle may be shallower than past soft markets, with insurers likely to reprice more sharply if large losses, inflation and capital signals deteriorate beyond expectations. Personal lines prices have started softening across major markets, Swiss Re said, noting that growth is shifting from rate-driven to volume-driven as insurers compete on price and terms rather than riding rate increases.

item.title: OneDegree promotes Emily Chow to chief executive
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Dhipaya braces for heavy claims after deadly Bangkok bar fire
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Guy Carpenter’s Cait Fisher embarks on new chapter in Bermuda
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Welcome To The ‘Show Me’ Era: Sapphire Ventures’ Anders Ranum On What Separates Winning AI Startups From The Rest
item.description: To understand how institutional capital is navigating this gap, Crunchbase News recently interviewed Anders Ranum , a partner at Sapphire Ventures . Meanwhile, private market valuations for AI startups continue to hit record highs. Ranum has spent nearly 15 years at the firm, where he focuses on B2B enterprise software, security and industrial infrastructure.

item.title: Cat loss retro rates fall up to 20% at mid-year renewals for loss-free accounts: Gallagher Re
item.description: Market conditions remained generally favourable for buyers of non-marine retrocession at the July renewals, according to reinsurance broker Gallagher Re, who highlights in its latest 1st View report, that catastrophe rates fell by up to 20% for loss free accounts. For non-marine retrocession, risk loss-free rates renewed -5% to -10% at July 1st, while catastrophe loss-free rates decreased by -10% to -20%. Backed by this healthy supply, buyers showed increased interest in expanding their aggregate and frequency protection at the mid-year renewals, as supply sufficiently met demand.

item.title: Ledger Investing reports continued demand for casualty ILS amid softening property cat market
item.description: Against the softening property catastrophe market, casualty remains the most focal alternative within the insurance-linked securities (ILS) space, but also the hardest to successfully close, according to Ledger Investing, who reports that while capital is actively seeking diversification, casualty rate adequacy remains intact, and the market is working deliberately to standardise. The insurtech and casualty ILS specialist detailed these dynamics in its recently published mid-year 2026 report, highlighting how the softening property market is creating further opportunities within the expanding casualty ILS sector. “Against a softening property-cat market, casualty remains the most-discussed alternative in ILS and, at the same time, the hardest to successfully close.

item.title: MSIG USA expands Board of Directors with three senior industry appointments
item.description: MSIG USA, the US specialty insurance arm of the global MS&AD Insurance Group, has expanded the Board of Directors of MSIG Holdings (USA), Inc. The three appointments bring a broad range of experience across finance, insurance regulation, technology and corporate strategy. Stafford joins the Board following a career spanning more than three decades in financial leadership, capital markets, governance and enterprise risk management.

item.title: AM Best maintains stable outlook on Guatemala’s insurance industry
item.description: Global credit rating agency AM Best is maintaining its stable outlook on Guatemala’s insurance industry, citing market expansion supported by economic growth and controlled inflation. “Guatemala’s insurance industry in 2025 maintained premium sufficiency, showing stability in claims and acquisition costs, demonstrating the market’s ability to navigate the different cycles in reinsurance,” said Inger Rodriguez, financial analyst, AM Best. According to the latest report, “Market Segment Outlook: Guatemala Insurance,” the country’s GDP growth is anticipated to moderate in 2026, though it will continue to be driven by private consumption and remittance inflows.

item.title: Gallagher Re appoints Michael Cipolla as VP, Facultative
item.description: Gallagher & Co., has announced the appointment of Michael Cipolla as Vice President, Facultative. Cipolla joins Gallagher Re from Everest, where he held several senior leadership roles, most recently serving as Casualty Chief Underwriting Officer of Everest Evolution, the dedicated U.S. Gallagher Re, the global reinsurance broking and advisory business of Arthur J.

item.title: Europe Battles Growing Wildfires After Relentless Heat
item.description: Wildfires are breaking out across Europe after three heat waves and weeks of low rainfall left much of the continent dangerously dry, with crews battling blazes south of Paris and in the UK, while Spain reels from one of its deadliest wildfires. While emergency services in southern France frequently tackle wildfires, it is rare to have such a large fire in the greater Paris region of Ile-de-France. Wildfires have burned around 320 square kilometers in France so far this year, roughly twice the area affected by this time last year, according to authorities.

item.title: Ryanair Passenger Partly Sucked From Jet After Window Breaks
item.description: jetliner made an unscheduled landing after a window cracked, two weeks after an accident on a separate plane in which a passenger was partially sucked out of a destroyed window and died. A Ryanair Holdings Plc flight was forced to make an emergency landing in Greece on Friday morning after a window on the Boeing Co. 737 dislodged soon after takeoff, causing panic on board after one passenger was reportedly partially sucked out of the cabin.

item.title: Low Water Hampers Rhine River Shipping in Germany, Transport Costs Rise
item.description: Low water levels in a current heatwave are preventing cargo vessels from sailing fully loaded on the Rhine river in Germany, increasing costs for freight transport, commodity traders said on Monday. Low water is hampering shipping on all the river south of Duisburg and Cologne, including the chokepoint of Kaub, traders added. Shallow water means vessel operators are imposing surcharges on freight rates to compensate for vessels not sailing fully loaded, increasing costs for cargo owners.

item.title: Dun & Bradstreet brings commercial insurance workflows to Claude
item.description: Dun & Bradstreet (D&B) has expanded its partnership with Anthropic by bringing commercial insurance workflows into Claude, enabling insurers and brokers to access business intelligence and risk data directly within AI-assisted underwriting processes. The integration connects D&B’s Commercial Graph and predictive analytics to Claude through Anthropic’s Model Context Protocol (MCP), allowing insurance professionals to automate several key commercial underwriting and compliance tasks. The insurance capabilities build on D&B’s broader risk and compliance integration with Claude launched last month, extending access to financial strength data, including business credit information, regulatory filings and payment history.

item.title: Jensten expands Scottish presence with Kelvin Smith Insurance acquisition
item.description: Jensten Group has agreed to acquire Glasgow-based Kelvin Smith Insurance, strengthening its footprint in Scotland with its third acquisition in the region this year. “Jensten was the natural choice for our next chapter, and we’re excited about the new opportunities this partnership brings for our people and our clients.” Robin Thomson, managing director at Jensten, said Kelvin Smith’s addition further strengthens the group’s Scottish platform. Founded in 1982, Kelvin Smith Insurance specialises in commercial insurance, fleet cover and SME risks, while also providing personal lines insurance for high-net-worth individuals.

item.title: Aviva updates cyber insurance offering
item.description: Aviva has refreshed its Cyber Complete and Cyber Respond products to expand coverage for businesses ranging from micro enterprises to mid-market companies. For Cyber Complete policies sold through Fast Trade and Acturis eTrade, Aviva increased the maximum indemnity limit from £2 million to £5 million and doubled the eligible turnover threshold to £100 million. Cyber Complete now includes clearer protection against AI-enabled attacks, deepfakes, digital impersonation, corporate identity fraud, social engineering scams, and the theft of client funds.

item.title: Sensible Weather partners with TCP Insurance
item.description: Sensible Weather, a startup offering coverage against bad weather, announced a strategic relationship with TCP Insurance, a provider of commercial entertainment and production insurance and part of Great American Insurance Group . Our parametric Weather Guarantees work hand-in-hand with traditional production insurance, helping ensure that when adverse weather impacts scheduled production days, crews are automatically reimbursed to help keep their productions on track.” – Nick Cavanaugh, Ph.D., CEO and Founder of Sensible Weather. “By combining our suite of commercial entertainment insurance products with Sensible’s innovative Weather Guarantees, we’re giving filmmakers, photographers and production companies an additional layer of financial confidence so they can stay focused on bringing their creative vision to life.

item.title: Farmers introduces simpler coverage tools and refreshed brand
item.description: Alongside the coverage tools, Farmers is refreshing its visual identity with simplified layouts, new typography, a high-contrast color palette, and a distinctive pink color. Farmers is introducing tools that help make insurance easier to navigate—highlighting what is and is not covered, offering coverage reviews even if your policy is with another insurer, and, in some cases, helping consumers explore alternatives when Farmers isn’t a fit for them.” – Eleanor Solomon, Head of Creative for Farmers Insurance. Farmers is rolling out new tools and communications designed to make its insurance policies easier for consumers to understand and compare.

item.title: Zurich Broadens Claims Capabilities to Support Construction Business
item.description: announced changes to its specialty claims structure to support its construction business. Three claims roles connected to Zurich’s construction business now report to Alberts: head of marine and energy, head of construction defect and head of construction professional liability. Alberts has 17 years of Zurich claims experience.

item.title: Former Pro Soccer Players Show Brain Changes But No Cognitive Decline
item.description: Retired British professional soccer players showed structural brain differences and high rates of anxiety and depression but no signs of cognitive decline in a study looking to establish if repetitive impacts like heading the ball affect the risk of developing dementia. Researchers found that the brain scans of the former players showed that, as a group, they had less brain tissue in areas controlling memory and emotion than the control group. The study, by researchers at Imperial College London, included 142 former players aged between 30 and 60 and compared them to 56 similarly aged healthy people with no history of contact sports, military service, or past concussions.

item.title: Xi Voices Worry on Work Safety After Shoe Factory Fire Kills 28
item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping voiced worries about repeated deadly workplace accidents after a blaze at a shoe factory killed 28 people. A major fire today broke out in a shoe factory in Jinjiang, Fujian, with significant casualties reported. The fire on Thursday happened at Fujian Huiteng Shoes Co.

item.title: Emergence Insurance nabs ex-cop Fraser Walker as New Zealand country head
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Marsh elevates 23-year veteran Robert Krleski to Pacific head of advocacy

item.title: OK Financial named preferred bidder for Yebyeol Non-Life

item.title: Best of Artemis, week ending July 12th 2026
item.description: Ten most read articles on Artemis.bm, week ending July 12th 2026: This is not every article published on Artemis during the last week, just the most popular among our readers over the last seven days. Here are the ten most popular news articles, week ending July 12th 2026, covering catastrophe bonds, ILS, reinsurance capital and related risk transfer topics. To ensure you never miss a thing subscribe to the weekly Artemis email newsletter updates or get our email alerts for every article we publish.

item.title: Hannover Re secures 60% upsized $200m 3264 Re 2026-1 retro cat bond
item.description: Hannover Re has now successfully priced its latest catastrophe bond sponsorship, securing a 60% upsized $200 million of additional North American peak peril retrocessional reinsurance from the new 3264 Re Ltd. Hannover Re tapped the cat bond market three times for nat cat protection in calendar year 2025, securing $200 million of worldwide peak peril retro through the 3264 Re 2025-1 cat bond, then $150 million of US and Canadian cover in May through the 3264 Re 2025-2 offering and finally another $250 million of North American peak peril retrocession through a 3264 Re Ltd. Hannover Re continues to build-out retro protection from the capital markets using catastrophe bonds and with the notes now priced, this will become the seventh sponsorship of a 3264 Re Ltd.

item.title: Nordic allocators increasingly see cat bonds as fixed income / alternatives complement: Markets Group
item.description: A new commentary by Markets Group, a company that specialises in forums for institutional allocators, has highlighted how Nordic allocators are increasingly looking at insurance-linked securities (ILS) and catastrophe bonds in particular as a complement to institutional fixed income and alternatives allocations. “Catastrophe bonds are being evaluated as a fixed income complement within institutional portfolios, with some allocators also considering them under an alternatives allocation. The commentary, authored by Kevin Gordon, a Research Manager at the firm, observes that Nordic institutional allocators are navigating a fixed income environment where traditional diversification is increasingly difficult to achieve.

item.title: QBE promotes Queralt Vila to Head of Distribution for Europe
item.description: International insurer and reinsurer, QBE Insurance Group Limited, has promoted Queralt Vila to Head of Distribution for Europe, effective August 1st, 2026, to lead the business and drive regional growth. Starting now, Vila will lead how QBE engages with brokers and clients across Europe, ensuring that distribution is fully aligned with its growth priorities. I am very happy to welcome her into the distribution team as I am positive our brokers and customers will appreciate her expertise and her energy.” Beatriz Valenti, Director of Insurance, QBE Europe, added, “The entire European team is very pleased to have Queralt supporting them in continuing to build stronger relationships with our partners, helping us achieve our ambitious growth targets.”

item.title: UK P&I Club and TT Club secure 89%+ shareholder backing for Thomas Miller acquisition
item.description: UK P&I Club and TT Club have secured shareholder acceptances exceeding 89% for their joint offer to acquire Thomas Miller Holdings, made on 18 June 2026 through TMH Bidco, a special-purpose vehicle established by the two clubs to facilitate the transaction. Jan Valkier, Chairman, UK P&I Club, commented, “We are encouraged by the strength of support that Thomas Miller’s shareholders have shown for the proposed acquisition offer. According to the two clubs, the acquisition of Thomas Miller is expected to be complete in Q4 2026, subject to the receipt of requisite regulatory approvals.

item.title: Arch Insurance International appoints Fred Bisson as Head of Digital Algorithms
item.description: Arch Insurance International, part of Arch Capital Group and a provider of specialty insurance solutions across multiple lines, has appointed Fred Bisson as Head of Digital Algorithms, effective immediately. His leadership will be invaluable as we continue to apply analytics in ways that deliver real value for our underwriters, brokers and clients.” Ed Short, Head of Digital, rch Insurance International, added, “Fred’s appointment reflects our continued investment in our digital capabilities and commitment to supporting brokers in their placement strategies. He will lead the development of algorithmic solutions to support digital trading capabilities In his new role, Bisson will develop and oversee algorithmic solutions to strengthen Arch’s digital trading capabilities across its international platform, spanning the London Market, UK Regional, Australia, EU and Bermuda.

item.title: Apple Sues OpenAI for Trade Secret Theft in Pivotal Case
item.description: sued OpenAI for trade secret theft, accusing the artificial intelligence startup and its hardware chief of engaging in a coordinated campaign to steal information about upcoming products. “At every level, from members of its technical staff to its chief hardware officer, and in coordination with business partners, OpenAI has been stealing Apple’s trade secrets and confidential information,” the Cupertino, California-based tech giant said in the suit. “As a natural result, OpenAI’s nascent hardware business now rests on the shakiest of foundations, rotten to its core by its illegal reliance on misappropriated trade secrets.” Apple said that Tan encouraged employees to provide information about upcoming products in job interviews.

item.title: New York Times Countersues EEOC Over DEI Lawsuit
item.description: The New York Times in a court filing on Friday accused the U.S. The countersuit filed in Manhattan federal court seeks to dismiss the EEOC’s lawsuit filed in May , a declaration that the agency’s actions violate the newspaper’s rights to free speech and due process under the U.S. Trump is also suing the Times in a $15 billion defamation lawsuit, accusing it of trying to sabotage his 2024 election campaign and disparage his reputation as a businessman.

item.title: AI rollout, war impacts to drive specialty innovation in greater China: Markel’s Chelsea Jiang

item.title: Ripe expands embedded golf insurance through Scottish Golf and Affordable Golf partnerships
item.description: The post Ripe expands embedded golf insurance through Scottish Golf and Affordable Golf partnerships appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . Golf Care, part of digital MGA Ripe Thinking , has expanded its UK distribution through two partnerships with Scottish Golf and golf equipment retailer Affordable Golf. Separately, Golf Care has launched what it describes as the UK’s first embedded insurance solution for golf retail with Affordable Golf.

item.title: Allianz Malaysia launches multi-year home insurance plan
item.description: Allianz Malaysia launched a multi-year version of its Smart Home Cover policy, offering three years of home insurance with a single upfront payment. With this campaign, the Smart Home Cover Multi-Year Plan not only ensures long-term protection for your home but also rewards our customers with an exclusive gift.” – Sean Wang, Chief Executive Officer of Allianz General. Customers choosing the plan receive lower premiums, avoid annual renewals, and get free reinstatement for the first two valid claims under the HouseOwner or HouseHolder sections.

item.title: Steadfast deal – devil is in the discount
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Buenas Fuentes: Galicia y Rappi se unen en Argentina
item.description: Galicia y Rappi impulsan finanzas embebidas en Argentina Galicia y Rappi integrarán soluciones de pago, crédito y bancarización dentro de la plataforma de delivery en Argentina. La alianza ofrecerá herramientas financieras a más de 15.000 comercios y 45.000 repartidores, ampliando la distribución de servicios mediante finanzas embebidas. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana.

item.title: Carmakers Broadly Win Round One in Massive UK Suits Over Diesel Emissions
item.description: Some of the world’s biggest carmakers on Friday largely won the first stage of a major legal battle over claims that their diesel vehicles were fitted with unlawful “defeat devices” to curtail emissions controls. In a summary of her judgment, Judge Sara Cockerill said she had “rejected most of the principal allegations advanced against the manufacturers whose vehicles were examined at trial.” She said that a defeat device under emissions regulation only covered “devices which operate with the intentional and/or impermissible purpose of causing the (emissions control system) to operate differently when it senses the test cycle.” The case centered on 20 sample vehicles made by Mercedes-Benz, Ford, Nissan, Renault and the Stellantis-owned brands Peugeot and Citroen. Cockerill did make some adverse findings, including over a coolant temperature device used in some Mercedes cars that was removed in a December 2015 update.

item.title: UK to Regulate Cloud Service Providers Microsoft, Google And Others to Protect Financial Stability
item.description: Britain has designated cloud service providers Microsoft, Google, Amazon and Oracle as critical third-party suppliers to its financial sector, bringing them under direct regulatory oversight. The government designated Microsoft Ireland Operations Ltd, Google Cloud EMEA Ltd, Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL, and Oracle Corporation UK Ltd as critical third parties, effective July 13. A Google Cloud spokesperson said: “With effective implementation and meaningful industry engagement, this new Critical Third Party framework can enhance the long-term resilience of the UK’s financial ecosystem and increase understanding, transparency, and trust between all parties.” (Reporting by Seers and Muvija M; Editing by William James and Mark Potter) Get the latest insurance news sent straight to your inbox.

item.title: Nearly 463,000 U.S. Kia Owners Warned to Park Outside Over Fire Risks
item.description: National Highway Traffic Safety Administration urged owners of 463,000 Kia crossover SUVs to park outdoors because of fire risks until new recall repairs are completed. The agency also urged owners to park outside after the initial recall in 2024. from the 2020 through 2024 model years because the front power seat motor may overheat, potentially causing a fire.

item.title: Month-long Speeding Awareness Campaign Aims to Reduce Fatalities
item.description: A new National Highway Traffic Safety Administration campaign highlights the dangers of speeding, a factor in nearly one-third of traffic deaths annually. In 2024, 11,288 people were killed in speeding-related crashes, accounting for 29% of all traffic fatalities, and an estimated 316,757 people were also injured. New preliminary estimates for 2025 project an 11% decline in speeding-related fatalities with an estimated 10,035 deaths.

item.title: Four Insurers Join Ward’s Annual Top 50 P/C Ranking for 2026
item.description: Property/casualty insurers have started to broadcast the fact that they earned spots on Ward’s list of 50 top-performing insurance companies for 2026, but only one was named for 36 straight years—RLI Corp. Ward, which provides benchmarking and best practices studies for the insurance industry, annually analyzes the financial performance for P/C and life insurers by reviewing five-year average measures of growth and profit, compiling lists of the top 50 carriers in each segment since 1991. According to Ward, a unit of Aon, the 50 P/C top performers for 2026 produced a 13.1 % statutory return on average equity from 2021 to 2025, compared to 8.6% for the P/C industry overall.

item.title: Logbook Lending Limited enters administration
item.description: On 1 July 2026, Logbook Lending Limited (trading as AFPremier.co.uk, pawnmy.co.uk, LBL Asset Finance, Log Book Loans 247) entered administration. Logbook Lending Limited provided lending secured on vehicles (known as logbook loans or bill of sale agreements), as well as pawnbroking. If you believe you have a claim against Logbook Lending Limited, or have any questions about your loan, the firm and Joint Administrators can be contacted on: Firm contact number: 020 8731 2557 Firm email: [email protected] Joint Administrators email: [email protected] Customers who are struggling financially can get free and impartial guidance from MoneyHelper .

item.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: A Pair Of Billion-Dollar Deals For Cyber And AI Infrastructure Lead
item.description: AI once again dominated venture funding this week, claiming five of the 10 largest announced rounds, including a pair of billion-dollar financings for AI infrastructure and cybersecurity that led the pack. This is a weekly feature that runs down the week’s top 10 announced funding rounds in the U.S. Check out last week’s biggest funding deal roundup here .

item.title: Porch secures debut $100m Harbor Crest Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond at lower pricing
item.description: Porch Group, the US homeowner’s specialist insurer has now priced its debut catastrophe bond, securing the targeted $100 million in multi-peril collateralized catastrophe reinsurance through the Harbor Crest Re Ltd. As a result, Porch Group has managed to successfully secure its targeted multi-peril collateralized catastrophe reinsurance limit at lower than anticipated pricing through its debut cat bond. In our first update on this deal, we reported that the size target had not been changed from the initial $100 million, however, Porch was looking to capitalise on investor appetites for risk by lowering the pricing of the notes on this Harbor Crest Re 2026-1 issuance.

item.title: Arch Insurance NA launches new US-based transactional liability team
item.description: Arch Insurance North America, part of Arch Capital Group Ltd., has expanded its Transactional Risk Insurance Product capabilities through the launch of a new U.S.-based transactional liability team. Anne Hardner, Executive Vice President, Executive Assurance for Arch Insurance, said, “Arch has been an active participant in the transactional risk market for more than 15 years through MGA and MGU partners and a variety of channels across North America, Bermuda and the United Kingdom. Their experience will help accelerate our growth while maintaining the disciplined approach that defines Arch.” Carson added, “Transactional risk insurance has become an important part of today’s M&A landscape, and Arch is uniquely positioned to deliver the financial strength, underwriting discipline and long-term commitment that brokers and clients increasingly value.
